mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns show no statistical correlation with FEDFUNDS changes.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weak consumer spending, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE rises above the 0.2pp threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates broad-based consumer demand recovery, which increases corporate revenue expectations and produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a baseline model with a t-statistic below 1.5 over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indexes reflect strengthening manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which translates to higher corporate earnings and positive S&P 500 forward returns over one month.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases consistently fail to predict positive 20-day returns during late-cycle expansion phases, with out-of-sample accuracy below 52%.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly CPI increase exceeding 0.4% triggers expectations of aggressive monetary tightening, which compresses equity valuations and generates negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent week.
failure_condition: SPX forward 5-day returns remain positive in over 60% of months where CPIAUCSL exceeds the 0.4% threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirms robust labor market expansion, boosting consumer confidence and driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The combined PAYEMS and UNRATE signal yields a negative or zero Rank IC over any 24-month rolling period.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real GDP exceeds 2.0%, signaling sustained economic expansion that supports corporate profit margins and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the following month.
failure_condition: GDP growth above 2.0% fails to predict positive 20-day returns with statistical significance (p-value > 0.10) during mid-cycle periods.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in the industrial production index exceeding 1.0% indicates a sharp contraction in manufacturing activity, which reduces near-term earnings forecasts and drives negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in more than 58% of instances where INDPRO falls below the 1.0% monthly decline threshold.
